name: Dollar Showdown
trigger: Extreme contrast forces resolution of which-is-better gap
why_it_works: A stark price gap makes the brain crave seeing whether more money actually delivers more.
examples: $1 vs $1,000,000 Hotel Room
$1 Pizza vs $1,000 Pizza
$10 Haircut vs $500 Haircut
Cheapest vs Most Expensive Car in the World
$5 Meal vs $5,000 Meal
Dollar Store Makeup vs Luxury Makeup

applied_examples.hook: $10 Gym vs $1,000 Gym: Which Builds More Muscle?
Free CRM vs $1,000/mo CRM: Is It Worth It?

name: Survival Stunt
trigger: Implied danger plus uncertain outcome demands the answer
why_it_works: An audacious endurance claim raises how-and-did-they-make-it questions only the video can answer.
examples: I Survived 50 Hours in a Maximum Security Prison
I Spent 7 Days in Solitary Confinement
I Survived 100 Hours in the Arctic
Last to Leave the Circle Wins $500,000
I Went 30 Days Without Talking
I Survived 24 Hours Underwater

applied_examples.hook: I Survived 7 Days With Zero Notifications

name: Oddly Specific Number
trigger: Concrete figures feel authentic and real, sparking why-that-number
why_it_works: A precise, weird number signals a real story and makes vague claims feel tangible and credible.
examples: Going Through the Same Drive Thru 1,000 Times
I Said Yes for 50 Hours Straight
I Counted to 100,000
I Planted 20,000,000 Trees
I Opened 10,000 Mystery Boxes
Watching 1,000 Sunrises in a Row

applied_examples.hook: I Flipped 1,000 Pancakes to Find the Perfect Recipe

name: Forward Reference
trigger: Vague pronoun withholds the referent, creating an information gap
template: You Won't Believe What [SUBJECT] Did Next
why_it_works: Definite articles and pronouns point at something undisclosed, so the brain must click to learn what 'it' is.
examples: You Won't Believe What Happens Next
This Is the One Thing That Changed Everything
Wait Until You See What She Found
The Reason Will Surprise You
This Changed How I See [TOPIC] Forever
What He Said Next Left Everyone Speechless

applied_examples.hook: This Buyer Made One Move That Saved $40,000

name: Nobody Talks About This
trigger: Implied secret omission makes viewer ask 'about what?'
why_it_works: Claiming a hidden, ignored truth triggers curiosity before the viewer consciously decides to stay.
examples: Nobody Talks About This, But...
No One Tells You This About [INDUSTRY]
Why Does Nobody Talk About [ANNOYING THING]?
The Thing No One Mentions About [TOPIC]
Everyone Ignores This One Detail
Nobody Talks About How Lonely Success Is

applied_examples.hook: Nobody Talks About the First 90 Days of Freelancing
No One Tells You This About the First Month With a Newborn

name: They Don't Want You to Know
trigger: Conspiracy framing implies forbidden, valuable hidden knowledge
template: What [AUTHORITY/INDUSTRY] Doesn't Want You to Know About [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Suggesting suppressed information makes the payoff feel exclusive and the gap urgent to close.
examples: They Don't Want You to Know This Secret
Here's What [EXPERTS] Won't Tell You
The Secret Billionaires Won't Post About
What They Hide From You About [TOPIC]
This Is the Trick Companies Don't Want Public
The Industry Doesn't Want This Out

name: Today Years Old
trigger: Shared late discovery promises a satisfying overlooked fact
template: I Was Today Years Old When I Learned [SURPRISING FACT]
why_it_works: Framing a fact as a just-now revelation implies it's surprising enough that you missed it too.
examples: I Was Today Years Old When I Found This Out
Apparently I've Been Doing This Wrong My Whole Life

TIL [SURPRISING FACT]
I Can't Believe I Just Learned This

name: Did You Know
trigger: Direct knowledge query opens a gap the viewer wants closed
why_it_works: A yes/no factual question surprises with knowledge most viewers don't have, compelling the reveal.

name: The Real Reason
trigger: Promised hidden cause overrides the assumed explanation
template: The Real Reason [OUTCOME] — It's Not What You Think
why_it_works: Dismissing the obvious explanation opens a gap that can only be closed by the true cause.
examples: The Real Reason People Fail — It's Not Talent
The Real Reason You're Always Tired
Why You're Stuck — and It's Not Motivation
The Actual Reason Your Videos Flop
It's Not What You Think — Here's the Real Cause
The Hidden Reason This Keeps Happening

applied_examples.hook: The Real Reason Leads Ghost You — It's Not Your Pitch

name: This Trick Famous Person Uses
trigger: Borrowed celebrity authority lends instant credibility
template: This Is the [METHOD] That [FAMOUS PERSON] Uses
why_it_works: Attaching a tactic to an admired figure transfers their success and authority to the claim.
examples: This Is the Routine Navy SEALs Swear By
The Note-Taking System Top CEOs Use
The Drill Olympic Sprinters Do Daily
This Is the Trick [Famous Investor] Lives By
How [Celebrity] Actually Stays Focused
The Habit Every Billionaire Seems to Share

applied_examples.hook: The Editing Trick Bestselling Authors Use on Every Draft

name: Imagine If
trigger: Vivid dream scenario activates aspiration and desire
why_it_works: Painting a vivid desirable future makes viewers crave the steps to actually get there.
examples: Imagine Waking Up to 12 New Clients Overnight
Imagine Never Worrying About [PROBLEM] Again
Imagine Finishing Your Work by Noon Every Day
Picture This: [DREAM SCENARIO]
What If Your [GOAL] Was Already Done by 9AM?
Imagine If [HARD THING] Felt Easy

applied_examples.hook: Imagine Waking Up to 50 Orders Before You Check Your Phone
